data.narrative:
Epsilon3 is a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Laura Crabtree that has raised $19M to date. The valuation analysis incorporates a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (ClearSpace at $400M, Hubble Network at $400M, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B), alongside five public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methods yielded applicable results. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next stage, using the $19M total raised as a proxy baseline, implying a $38M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector/stage private companies yields a median valuation of $850M, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring (median P/S of 42.9x across the five public tickers) was not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $444M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Epsilon3's commercial traction and technology differentiation align with the lower-stage comps (ClearSpace, Hubble Network) or the later-stage, more mature peers (Aalyria, Cowboy Space). Additional clarity on revenue trajectory, customer contracts, and technology maturity would narrow this range substantially.
